etymology.description: semantic 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") + phonetic 屯(屯) (tún)
etymology.interpretation: The character combines the semantic element of 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread"), representing something fine and uniform like a singular, untainted thread, with the phonetic 屯(屯) (tún) to provide the pronunciation. Together, these components convey the sense of something being 'pure' or 'without impurities,' emphasizing the idea of uniformity and exclusivity in purity.
etymology.simplification: Simplified by reducing 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") to 纟(纟), which is a simpler form used in several characters to maintain the semantic implication of 'silk; thread.'

examples.chinese: 这个水看起来很纯(這個水看起來很純)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège shuǐ kàn qǐlái hěn chún.

examples.chinese: 她有一颗纯真的心(她有一顆純真的心)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǒu yī kē chúnzhēn de xīn.

examples.chinese: 为了保持纯净(為了保持純淨)，不能往茶里加糖(不能往茶裡加糖)。
examples.pinyin: Wèile bǎochí chúnjìng，bùnéng wǎng chá lǐ jiā táng.
examples.english: To keep it pure, you shouldn't add sugar to the tea.
